Recipe: Pyro Smoke / Fire Simulation
content:
INTENT: create a pyro smoke or fire simulation
PHASES:
  1. create_node geo /obj pyro_source
  2. create_node_chain /obj/pyro_source:
     - {name: sphere_src, type: sphere, parms: {radx:0.2, rady:0.2, radz:0.2}}
     - {name: emit_pts, type: scatter, parms: {npts:500}}
     - {name: OUT, type: null, inputs:[emit_pts]}
  3. create_node dopnet /obj pyro_dopnet
  4. Inside /obj/pyro_dopnet, create: pyro_solver (type:pyrosolver), source_volume (type:sourcevolume connected to /obj/pyro_source/OUT)
  5. Wire: source_volume → pyro_solver
VERIFY: dopnet has pyrosolver, volume SOP attached as source
REPAIR: ensure source_volume CHOP input points to emitter geo, not empty

title: Recipe: FLIP Fluid Simulation
content:
INTENT: create a FLIP fluid simulation
PHASES:
  1. create_node geo /obj flip_source
  2. create_node_chain /obj/flip_source:
     - {name: basin, type: box, parms: {sizex:2, sizey:0.5, sizez:2, ty:0.25}}
     - {name: fluid_vol, type: bound, inputs:[basin]}
     - {name: OUT, type: null, inputs:[fluid_vol]}
  3. create_node dopnet /obj flip_dop
  4. Inside flip_dop: flipsolver, flipobject (source=/obj/flip_source/OUT), staticobject (for container)
  5. Wire: flipobject + staticobject → flipsolver
VERIFY: dopnet cooks without errors, particle count > 0 at frame 2
REPAIR: if 'diverge/NaN', reduce substeps or lower velocity; ensure collider SDF is closed mesh

title: Recipe: RBD Destruction / Fracture
content:
INTENT: fracture and simulate an RBD destruction effect
PHASES:
  1. create_node geo /obj rbd_geo
  2. create_node_chain /obj/rbd_geo:
     - {name: base_box, type: box, parms: {sizex:2, sizey:2, sizez:2}}
     - {name: fracture, type: voronoifracture, inputs:[base_box], parms: {npts:50}}
     - {name: OUT, type: null, inputs:[fracture]}
  3. create_node dopnet /obj rbd_dop
  4. Inside rbd_dop: rbdpackedobject (source=/obj/rbd_geo/OUT), rbdsolver, groundplane
  5. Wire: rbdpackedobject + groundplane → rbdsolver
VERIFY: pieces separate on simulation, ground catches them
REPAIR: if pieces stuck, check constraint network is disconnected; reduce 'glue strength'

title: Recipe: Vellum Cloth Simulation
content:
INTENT: simulate cloth with Vellum
PHASES:
  1. create_node geo /obj cloth_geo
  2. create_node_chain /obj/cloth_geo:
     - {name: grid_cloth, type: grid, parms: {sizex:2, sizez:2, rows:20, cols:20}}
     - {name: vellum_setup, type: vellumsource, inputs:[grid_cloth], parms: {clothtype:0}}
     - {name: OUT, type: null, inputs:[vellum_setup]}
  3. create_node dopnet /obj vellum_dop
  4. Inside vellum_dop: vellumsolver, vellumobject (source=/obj/cloth_geo/OUT), vellumdraganddamp
  5. Wire: vellumobject → vellumsolver → vellumdraganddamp
VERIFY: cloth falls and drapes under gravity
REPAIR: if cloth explodes, reduce substeps or increase constraint iterations

title: Recipe: Attrib Wrangle VEX — Set Cd by Height
Recipe: Attrib Wrangle VEX — Random Pscale
content:
INTENT: randomise pscale on scattered points
TOOL: create_node attribwrangle inside SOP network
VEX:
  @pscale = fit01(rand(@ptnum), 0.05, 0.3);
PARMS: class=point, snippet=<above vex>
NOTE: fit01 maps 0..1 rand to 0.05..0.3; adjust range to taste
VERIFY: copy_to_points downstream shows varied instance sizes
REPAIR: if all same size, confirm runover=points not primitives

title: Recipe: Create and Promote HDA
content:
INTENT: wrap a SOP network into an HDA
PHASES:
  1. Select target subnet node in /obj/geo
  2. RMB → Create Digital Asset → set operator name, label, save path (.hda)
  3. In Type Properties, add parameters and wire them to internal parms via 'Relative References'
  4. Save HDA
TOOL CALLS:
  - create_hda(node_path='/obj/geo/mynet', operator_name='myasset', hda_path='$HIP/myasset.hda')
  - promote_parm(hda_path=..., internal_path='box1/sizex', parm_name='Width')
VERIFY: HDA appears in TAB menu, parameters visible on node
REPAIR: if parm not promoted, check channel reference uses `chs('../Width')` syntax
